concerns.text: FR-004 (exploratory data analysis with scatter plots) is not referenced or implemented in any phase of the plan. This creates an orphan FR.

SC-002 (model selection based on goodness-of-fit metrics) is absent from the plan; the plan only mentions SC-005 for multicollinearity but does not cite SC-002 for model-selection decisions. This creates an orphan SC.

The specification lacks a formal, verified dataset citation (e.g., a ‘Verified datasets’ block with the canonical URL https://katlas.org) for the Knot Atlas dataset. Without this citation the dataset cannot be validated by the deterministic resolver.
User Story 2 (Establish Measurement Precision for Core Invariants) is not linked to any functional requirement (FR-###) or success criterion (SC-###). This creates an orphan story.

The `KnotRecord` entity in the specification’s Key Entities section defines only a `source` object and omits the required top-level fields `source_timestamp` and `checksum_sha256` that the `knot_record.schema.yaml` contract mandates. This mismatch will cause schema validation failures.

The contract `knot_record.schema.yaml` expects a nested `source` object with fields `database`, `version`, `url`, `accessed_at`. The specification’s `KnotRecord` definition also lists `source_timestamp` and `checksum_sha256` as separate top-level fields, but does not embed them inside the `source` object as required, creating a schema inconsistency.

Because the specification’s `KnotRecord` entity both adds `source_timestamp` and `checksum_sha256` (top-level) and expects them inside the `source` object per the contract, there is a contradictory definition that will prevent records from validating against the contract.

FR-005 describes regression models with crossing number and braid index as predictors, but acknowledges the braid index is mathematically constrained by b ≤ c. This violates the independence assumption required for meaningful coefficient interpretation. The specification should acknowledge this limitation more explicitly.
SC-013 states that Knot Atlas and KnotInfo may share underlying data sources. If they do, the cross-check is not independent validation. The specification should explicitly state this limitation.
